summary.intent: Migrate Pipelines-as-Code authentication from GitHub PAT to GitHub App credentials on an OpenShift/Konflux cluster. The pipelines-as-code-secret (kubernetes.io/basic-auth) is used by manually-triggered PipelineRuns for git-clone operations.
summary.outcome: Researched architecture and identified that PAC's secret-auto-create only works for webhook-triggered runs, not manual runs. Proposed two approaches: art-tools patching the secret before each run, or a CronJob periodically refreshing it. User rejected Option A due to concurrent build race conditions and wants to explore the CronJob approach.

repo: ART uses manually-triggered PipelineRuns via konflux_client.py, not PAC webhooks
PAC is explicitly disabled for ART components via build.appstudio.openshift.io/status annotation
The pipelines-as-code-secret is mounted as git-auth workspace into git-clone-oci-ta and prefetch-dependencies-oci-ta tasks
artcd-task.yaml already mounts github-app-credentials secret with GITHUB_APP_ID and GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH
github_auth.py in artcommon already has GitHub App installation token generation logic with PAT fallback
Two distinct secrets named pipelines-as-code-secret exist: one in PAC controller namespace, one in build namespaces

code.finding: PAC is explicitly disabled via annotation: build.appstudio.openshift.io/status with pac.state=disabled

workflow: PAC's secret-auto-create feature only triggers for webhook-generated PipelineRuns, not manually created ones
GitHub App installation tokens expire after 1 hour, requiring continuous refresh unlike static PATs
Tekton git-clone-oci-ta task requires kubernetes.io/basic-auth format with username/password fields, cannot use App credentials directly
KonfluxClient already has CoreV1Api imported for Kubernetes secret operations

friction: Multiple concurrent builds would race to patch the same secret if refreshed at build time
PAC's native GitHub App support doesn't work for manually-triggered PipelineRuns
1-hour token expiration requires active refresh mechanism vs. static PAT approach
open_items: Design and implement CronJob-based secret refresh mechanism on the cluster
Determine optimal CronJob frequency (30-45 min suggested to avoid 60min expiration)
Configure RBAC permissions for CronJob to patch secrets in build namespace
Mount GitHub App credentials (app-id and private-key) into CronJob pod
Handle token refresh timing window to prevent expiration gaps between CronJob runs
